neutral.parent: We honestly never got that far — Periscope came out around the same time and sucked all the wind out of the sails. We pivoted to a communication platform for local newsrooms, enabling producers and editors (i.e., people in the building) to geospatially understand where their crews were and communicate with them more effectively. Local news is a pretty shitty market, though 😅 not awash in cash

positive.comment: great question. first, we do allow non-livestream posts direct from your camera so you can create 30 second video clips on the map to help people discover your content and make the app to be useful in a standalone way, we created layer communities to help you group content on the map and invite your friends and share it directly to other platforms we also have a popular feed in the app, which will show your posts to more people if it gets a lot of engagement, helping reward people who make content that sticks
you can save 30 seconds videos in the app, but we don't currently save livestreams. we'd love to do this in the near future though

positive.comment: If the goal is truly a public service in the aim of doing good, I would suggest bulletproofing yourself and the site against things that could sway or make you fragile. For example, adding ads means that you are now working for advertisers and won’t want to upset them. There are some really nifty open source libraries out there that can run live streams in almost decentralized fashions. Then, I think you can host the streams for a small amount of time before ejecting them or database dumping them somewhere where other people can torrent them The name of the game is keeping your hosting costs low and renaming anti-fragile in the face of adversity if you’re building a site around free speech and accountability I’m a senior software engineer in California. Happy to help if you’d like

positive.comment: Something you can try is, Tell ChatGPT about your product and ask it to generate 50 at a time, brandable .com domain name ideas. Probably most of the .com domain names with "live" keywords are taken, but maybe you can be a little more creative.

neutral.comment: You should definitely try and get those data layers to a CDN cache and serve them for pennies on the Edge instead of serving everything from your main host. Highly distributed apps, especially ones like yours will demand a lot of data at critical mass, and that will be stupid money if you try and serve egress from a hosting provider. Checkout something like Cloudflare R2.
neutral.parent: we're on digitalocean! so far just paying a few hundred bucks a month

neutral.comment: Hello! I am OP's partner/co-creator of this app. In short, we do not collect any data that is not strictly necessary for the correct functioning of the app. When creating an account, all we ask for is an email and username/password; no gender/birthday/country/etc. When posting, we obviously need location permission to be able to show the location of the event/point of interest. But we do not care about your location otherwise (we do not track continuously). So, in theory, one could remain anonymous if taking the right precautions (we allow signing up with google, so if anonymity is a priority, maybe don't use this method.)
neutral.parent: Can you prevent certain agencies from using this as self collected data on the protest layer?The news does a great job picking and choosing video bites to show a narrative with 1 or 2 cameras covering the events… can you ensure that our own videos won’t be clipped down to tell the news narrative? If so, you’ve broken away from the BS that you were sick of. If not, you’ve given them more ammunition to pick through. What data is collected? Can a user remain anonymous while streaming so no data collected can be associated with a single person if their face is never shown?

positive.comment: about 3 years of very intense effort lol. the first year or two i was building this i was regularly staying up until 6-7am working on it and then waking up at 930 for my regular engineering job ive basically had no life for the past 3 years so im super glad we released finally :D
positive.parent: how long did it take to code all this ?

positive.comment: so our team consistents of myself and my partner. i handle all the engineering, partner handles product direction / marketing / business / etc and he had the idea of doing a map based app. he's one of the smartest & wisest people ive ever worked with. from the engineering perspective i can say this was the most intense thing ive ever worked on. around 3 yrs of effort, i regularly stayed up until 7am working on it and woke up 930am for my regular engineering job multiple times a week. basically have had not much of a life for several years now but this was highly collaborative - my partner and i would bounce ideas off each other all the time, and refine how we did things. tech stack is python & react native early intuition was that we wanted to crowdsource location data to inform people. we thought it would be nice to go out and be notified about stuff your vicinity. the ideas evolved rather radically from there, into the concept of map-based communities and livestreaming
positive.parent: Man this is so awesome! It could literally change the world with its potential for showing what events unfolded and where with pin point accuracy. positive.comment: Hello! I am OP's partner/co-creator of this app. I am sure people will find ways, geospoofing is still a concern, but we hope more people will use the app in legitimate ways and help police bad actors. Users can report offensive/graphic posts, and these get blurred out automatically once a reasonable threshold of reports is reached.
positive.parent: Love this! Very relevant nowadays. I'm sure people still would find a way to fake it somehow (i.e. pointing the camera at a display or something?). But very good idea. Love it!

positive.comment: Hello! I am OP's partner/co-creator of this app. We have features in the roadmap that would meet this need in a way. Requesting livestreams for money could be a sensitive issue since it borders on “pay-per-surveillance”, which is not something we want to do. If the app continues to gain traction, we want to introduce features that would allow for better organization around events, but right now we have our hands full. Thank you for your suggestion!
